title: Going Nuts for Chocolate Covered Treats!
story: Chocolate covered nuts are having their moment to shine on February 25! People celebrate National Chocolate Covered Nut Day by munching on their favorite nutty treats dipped in creamy chocolate. Grocery stores stock up on chocolate covered peanuts, almonds, cashews, and hazelnuts for this sweet holiday. Many families make their own by melting chocolate and dipping fresh nuts at home. Candy shops create special displays featuring every type of chocolate covered nut you can imagine. Social media fills with photos of people's favorite combinations, from dark chocolate covered walnuts to milk chocolate peanuts. It's the perfect excuse to enjoy this crunchy, sweet combo!
activity_question: What do many families do at home to celebrate this nutty holiday?
activity_choices: Plant nut trees in their backyard
Melt chocolate and dip fresh nuts
Write poems about their favorite nuts
activity_answer: Melt chocolate and dip fresh nuts
bonus_fact: Chocolate covered nuts were first sold commercially in the 1800s!

title: Slurp It Up: National Clam Chowder Day
story: Bowls of creamy, steaming clam chowder take center stage every February 25 on National Clam Chowder Day! Restaurants across America serve up their best recipes, from thick New England white chowder to tangy Manhattan red chowder. Families gather around dinner tables to enjoy this hearty soup filled with tender clams, potatoes, and vegetables. Food lovers post pictures of their favorite bowls online and debate which style tastes better.
activity_question: What are the two main types of clam chowder mentioned in the story?
activity_choices: New England white and Manhattan red
activity_answer: New England white and Manhattan red
follow_up_question: What ingredients would you put in your perfect clam chowder recipe?

title: Inconvenience Yourself Day: Making Life Harder on Purpose
story: February 25 is Inconvenience Yourself Day, when people purposely make their lives a little harder to appreciate what they usually take for granted! Some folks walk instead of driving, eat with their non-dominant hand, or take cold showers. Others might skip their phone for a few hours or do chores the old-fashioned way. It's a quirky reminder to be grateful for modern conveniences we use every day without thinking twice!
activity_question: What might someone do on Inconvenience Yourself Day?
activity_choices: Buy the most expensive items at the store
follow_up_question: What modern convenience would be hardest for you to give up for a day?
